title: Move model.type behavior (ratios, qualityTiers) into ModelDef — shrink the add-model sweep
description: REFACTOR (from 2026-07-02 complexity audit; ~3h, low risk, additive). Adding a model with a NEW model.type is a documented 10-file sweep (docs/add-model-playbook.md §6 calls it a trap; bit wan5b). Cause: behavior contracts stored OUTSIDE the model definition — js/utils/ratios.js getModelRatios() switch (~line 228) + RATIO_MODES, QUALITY_TIERS_BY_MODEL in js/components/Compounds/MpiOptionSelector/MpiOptionSelector.js, tiersFor in js/migrations/projectMigrations.js. FIX: add optional `ratios` + `qualityTiers` fields to ModelDef in js/data/modelConstants/models.js; consumers check `model.ratios ?? getModelRatios(model.type)` (fallback keeps existing models unchanged). New models then declare behavior at registration — sweep drops from 10 files to 7. Update docs/add-model-playbook.md after. Mismatches currently fail SILENTLY (phantom tiers / wrong ratio table). BRANCH: RunPod.
